key_insight: A model-agnostic audit combining explainability, counterfactual checks, and DFT verification can expose both spurious learned correlations and wrong training labels in materials-property prediction.
engineering_relevance: For spin Hall conductivity screening, this enables cheaper composition-only models to be used at scale while still detecting when predictions or labels are physically unreliable.
method: Use SHAP and partial dependence to test whether features track physics or training-set artifacts.
Cross-check multiple models with Rashomon-style verification to see if explanations are stable.
Validate suspicious findings with targeted DFT calculations on selected compounds.
evidence_signal: On intrinsic spin Hall conductivity, the composition-only Random Forest matched structure-aware model performance, but DFT showed a Pt-free compound was underpredicted by nearly 4× and revealed a 30× error in the HfC training label.
watch_item: The audit still depends on a small number of targeted DFT calculations, so its reliability is limited by the quality and coverage of those first-principles checks.
paper_title: Auditing Machine-Learning Models and Their Training Data with Explainability and First-Principles Verification: Application to Spin Hall Conductivity
